assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: The response mentions that slices become unavailable 'simultaneously or in sequence' but doesn't clearly explain that TPU slices are recreated atomically as a single unit, not through rolling upgrades

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: The response clearly states 'even if only one slice needs maintenance, the whole distributed training will stop' and 'Your entire multislice job will be terminated'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: The response clearly states that the entire multislice job will be terminated and that the job won't automatically resume, indicating all pods restart and training is interrupted

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: The response mentions 'Maintenance can last several hours' but doesn't specifically explain the resource availability blocking issue or potential for extended downtime due to insufficient TPU resources

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: The response explicitly recommends 'Force immediate checkpoint' and provides a kubectl command example for triggering checkpointing before maintenance

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: The response includes 'Automated Recovery Plan' section with automatic job resubmission and checkpoint restoration, addressing automatic resume functionality

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: The response does not mention maintenance exclusions or the option to defer maintenance events for training runs close to completion

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: The response does not discuss maintenance windows or scheduling options to control when maintenance occurs during planned training breaks
